title: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan

seo.title: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan: Authentic & Easy Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the authentic flavors of Sichuan with our Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan recipe. Spicy, savory, and easy to make at home. Get the best tips for a perfect dish!

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan with peanuts, dried chilies, and Sichuan peppercorns
seo.imageCaption: Savor the bold and spicy flavors of our homemade Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan, a true Chinese classic.

description: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan is a fiery and flavorful stir-fry, renowned for its perfect balance of spicy, sweet, sour, and savory tastes. This iconic dish features tender chicken pieces, crunchy peanuts, and a signature kick from dried chilies and Sichuan peppercorns, creating a truly addictive eating experience. It is a beloved classic that embodies the vibrant spirit of Sichuan cuisine.

ingredients.notes: white and green parts separated, sliced

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the cubed chicken with 1 tbsp cornstarch and 1 tbsp soy sauce. Mix well until the chicken is evenly coated. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or refrigerate for up to 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Kung Pao Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the rice wine vinegar, dark soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, sugar, chicken broth, sesame oil, and 1 tsp cornstarch. Set aside. This is your flavorful sauce.

instructions.title: Stir-fry Aromatics and Chilies
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add the dried red chilies and Sichuan peppercorns. Stir-fry for 30 seconds to 1 minute until fragrant and the chilies are slightly darkened. Be careful not to burn them.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the marinated chicken to the wok, spreading it out in a single layer. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes, breaking up any clumps, until the chicken is mostly cooked through and lightly browned. Remove the chicken from the wok and set aside.

instructions.title: Add Ginger, Garlic, and Scallions
instructions.text: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok. Add the minced garlic, minced ginger, and the white parts of the sliced scallions. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish the Dish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the wok. Give the prepared Kung Pao sauce a quick whisk to recombine any settled cornstarch, then pour it over the chicken. Stir quickly to coat everything, cooking until the sauce thickens and coats the chicken, about 1-2 minutes. Stir in the roasted peanuts and the green parts of the scallions. Serve immediately.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, ensure your Sichuan peppercorns are fresh and potent. Toasting them briefly in a dry pan before adding to the oil enhances their numbing sensation (ma la).
Velveting the chicken with cornstarch and soy sauce creates a tender, silky texture that is characteristic of Chinese stir-fries. Do not skip this step for the best results.

Serve Kung Pao Chicken immediately with steamed white rice to best enjoy the contrast of the spicy, savory sauce and the fresh, soft rice. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan is a beloved Chinese Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
